item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor guy.

+!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To say this guy has a death wish is a understatement.
However, to say this guy treats his life as a long-running joke would be spot on.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would work as a J, but it has more charm as a scp file. The fact they are trying their damnest so the guy doesn't get killed is amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He is the Living Avatar of the Darwin Award, made flesh and set to walking the world like a man!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did I downvote this

This is hilarious

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know what emotions I should be feeling, but I mean that in a good way. Should I laugh or be sad for him? I am conflicted. At least he seems okay with it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The polygraph is obviously 1531, which is true.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just "What if a really stupid guy got the ability to reincarnate so that I could write a log of a lot of stupid deaths?" Admittedly, some were funny, but ultimately… downvoted for a silly concept that just stayed silly. I like the silly concepts when they are more than just the silly concept. This is just a silly concept.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Yep!

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Now that the MTF contest is over, can I go back and make those edits?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted not just because of the funnies in it, but more or less for the connections to Flowers for Algernon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Last words were reportedly "gimme a kiss"

+OVER 9000!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well on one hand this is amusing, on the other hand it feels underdeveloped and the logic behind the Omicron-8 role isn't clear to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does the Foundation know this and decide to use Omicron-8 though?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that was thoroughly enjoyable. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this one, it's hilarious! I like how you turned a simple concept of a person dying and coming back to something cool like this, I can't even describe it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article cracked me up while being just serious enough to not be a -J.
+1

One nitpick I have, however, is that seizures usually aren't fatal unless you whack your head really good falling down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On the lie detector, they're not total bunk. Its similar to hypnotism- it doesn't work on everyone, and isn't as easy or reliable as Hollywood thinks, and relies a lot on the skill of the practitioner and the susceptibility of the subject… but it does work to some extent on most people, and extremely well on a few people. It's just that, like witness testimony itself, it isn't something that can really be relied on as hard evidence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looks like the point I was trying to make didn't come across, which is on me.

To clarify, for you and others who didn't get it: SCP-2416 does not have a mental disability of any kind. He has great capacity for intelligence, and chooses not to use it for whatever reason. Think about the title - the laws of physics and the history of Earth is a story, and Joe Grunderson-Pike is the comic relief. He has possibly been around since time immemorial.

He's a clown. He exists to die for others' amusement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My overall feeling probably allies with others in that it doesn't quite meet clinical tone. The entire log and particularly the interview read like vaudeville patter, and I don't know if the actual containment/description are consequential or grounded enough to balance that. It is funny though.

(If that's a running meta-joke with Send in the Clowns, I haven't read enough to get it.)

Minor things that stuck out:
1) It's a bit odd that you just straight from text into the log into the interview without titles. It's not earth-shaking, but sort of indicative of the whole lack-of-clinical/Foundation-tone issue.
2) Unless the Foundation is using some kind of anomalous/paratech lie detector, they really shouldn't be using polygraphs. They're notoriously pseudoscientific bunk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the things that I don't understand is why Omicron-8 is involved in containment here. They specialise in humorous anomalies, yes, and this is funny because it appears slapstick and zany to the wiki reader, but is this humorous in-universe? Did the Foundation look at this man, dying over and over again in painful ways, and clearly suffering from a mental illness / cognitive disability, and think "that's hilarious, let's put the joke guys on it"? That either makes the Foundation particularly unfeeling, or exceptionally meta-aware, and either answer breaks my suspension of disbelief in the MTF's purpose.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I forgot this was a thing. :c

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: This is the correct move. Feel free to incorporate these changes once the contest is over, however. And if you wish to make small corrections, this is also fine (small corrections are described in more detail as per the rules page).

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Myeah, on the basis of the rule quoted in the above post, I've reverted your edit. It is too extensive to count as a small correction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You may not delete or edit an article once posted, regardless of performance… What's posted is posted. Small corrections are fine, but significant edits are not.

From the contest page. Maybe I'm just reading the rules wring, but isn't this disallowed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Making some edits on these points. ALLONS-Y!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I think you need to go more slapstick and over-the-top.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I damn near woke up my roommate laughing. Upvoted, but seconding the comments about fixing tone and stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quite a few problems with this.

+ show block
– hide block

How does the police, then the Foundation, know that it's always te same guy reappearing after each death? Not clear!

If most of the circumstances of his death are known, including the reasons ('was tired', 'gimme a kiss' and so on) there must have been witnesses. How come no one is ever able to prevent him from killing himself?

If SCP-2416-21 dies for any reason, Mobile Task Force Omicron-8 "Send In The Clowns" is to be placed on high alert, and the replacement instance is to be apprehended and contained in the same manner as soon as possible.

How can they do this before he dies?

and a high likelihood of getting into a lethal accident

Isn't it more like a 100% likelihood?

Interviewer: Dr. Ichinose

Nothing in Tobias' tale Finfiction indicates that Shiki Ichinose is a doctor… Or is it another Ichinose?
(I like the name, though… ;>)

What Scorpion says:

(ie. "shot while trying to befriend Chilean anarchists" is plausible stupidity, "last words were reportedly "gimme a kiss"." not so much)

I second heartily!


OTOH:

SCP-2416-8 [REDACTED] The badgers suffered only minor injuries.

I love this. It's subtle and very funny!

Okay. I don't know what to do.
Here, I'll let the badgers prevent me from downvoting! ;>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know what, Scorpion, I think I'm going to print the second paragraph of your post above, frame it and put it up in pride of place on one of my walls! ;>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a little too on the nose to be a mainlister in my opinion, but it's not bad. No vote for now. Also, isn't the COD from acetominophen overdose usually kidney failure?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agree with the other commenters- while not the most original idea, the "complete moron" aspect gives it just that little bit of needed novelty. I think turning the dial down from "zany" to "tongue in cheek" would vastly improve this (ie. "shot while trying to befriend Chilean anarchists" is plausible stupidity, "last words were reportedly "gimme a kiss"." not so much)

Remember when you're working on Bathos/High Wierdness-type stuff that its just like horror- push either one too far and you fall over the cliff into narm. A few subtle gags with a lot of buildup and deadpan counterweight will get you exponentially more effect than just throwing everything you can think of at the page and hoping that the stuff that works makes up for the stuff that doesn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it very much, up until the interview, which changed it from "funny but compelling" to overly silly. Remove it or make the dialogue somewhat less cartoonish and I'd gladly upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The reincarnacion guy its been done a lot, but Im willing to let that pass. The log was very good, its certantly the best part of the article, but the whole thing its eehhh… Appart from the log, its very weak. The tone could use some work
"This was not believed to have been suicide" That bit particulary sounds off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The main problem is it's one of many "guy comes back to life" skips. It's been done. This differentiates itself somewhat by the ridiculous ways this guy keeps dying, but as Deci said, it's a little too ridiculous. My one question is, is he aware of his reincarnating ability and just doing dumb shit because he knows he'll come back?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the one hand, I don't mind the concept & idea here. On the other, I'm not sure about how it was executed. The log of instances is admittedly pretty funny (and I think this could actually work as a mainlister, really; it makes plausible sense in-universe), but the document itself doesn't seem to read too clinically, and the article presented here almost reads like it's pitching an idea instead of a full, finished concept.

I wanna like it, but I dunno how to feel about this.

EDIT: While I'm downvoting ultimately, keep this up for the rest of the contest, if only to get more feedback on how to improve it. You're getting quite a bit of good feedback here and this could be salvaged really nicely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to TobiasTheTapir and MrWrong for help on this contest entry.
